mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% in 60%+ of cases, or the correlation between CPI and returns falls below -0.15 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals strong supply-side momentum, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in 50%+ of instances, or their combined predictive power (Rank IC) drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) reduce liquidity and raise borrowing costs, creating headwinds for equity markets and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% in 60%+ of cases, or the correlation between rate hikes and returns falls below -0.20 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) reflects tightening labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate margins, which drive positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative when UNRATE declines in 50%+ of instances, or the correlation between UNRATE and returns falls below 0.10 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains exceeding 100k (PAYEMS) signal robust job creation, boosting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain negative when PAYEMS exceeds 100k in 50%+ of cases, or the correlation between payroll growth and returns falls below 0.15 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative when HOUST rises in 50%+ of instances, or the correlation between housing starts and returns falls below 0.10 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) reflects broad-based demand expansion, driving corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in 50%+ of instances, or their combined predictive power (Rank IC) drops below 0.05.
